mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer income and spending, leading to increased corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market strength supports consumption growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS changes fail to lead to positive returns in more than 50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a neutral level of 2.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns after FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) over multiple tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising CPI inflation and declining unemployment signals an overheating economy that prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, leading to negative forward returns over the following 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when both inflation and employment are high
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when trigger is active, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low and stable inflation (CPI YoY less than 2%), rising industrial production signals real economic growth without inflationary pressures, boosting equity markets with positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during low-inflation periods, positive INDPRO changes do not lead to positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (Rank IC > 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate robust construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to higher economic growth and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST month-over-month increases are not followed by positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5%, the labor market is considered tight, which may lead to wage inflation and Fed tightening, causing negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day returns after UNRATE crosses below 5% are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces industrial production growth, demand exceeds supply, leading to inventory drawdowns and future production increases, which is positive for stocks over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the triggered signal fails to produce positive returns with frequency > 55% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation regime (CPI YoY greater than 3%), an increase in the federal funds rate is particularly detrimental to equities because it validates inflation fears and increases discount rates sharply, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during high inflation, positive FEDFUNDS changes do not lead to negative 20-day returns with Rank IC < -0.03.
